name: Bobby Timmons
abstract: Robert Henry Timmons (December 19, 1935 – March 1, 1974) was an American jazz pianist and composer. He was a sideman in Art Blakey's Jazz Messengers for two periods (July 1958 to September 1959; February 1960 to June 1961), between which he was part of Cannonball Adderley's band.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: Who have you got in mind for piano? I said, 'A fellow who used to play with Chet Baker. He could play hip. He could play funky. He’s great.' He said, 'What’s his name?' I said, 'Bobby Timmons.' ... So we got a new band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Israels
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Bobby Timmon’s trio opposite Monk’s quartet at the Five Spot in New York for weeks and weeks.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Israels interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: So this night I was appearing there with Hank Mobley, tenor saxophone, and Booker Little, trumpet, Bobby Timmons, bass and Sam Jones — Bobby Timmons piano and Sam Jones bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: In the early 60’s he drove the combos of Sonny Rollins, Bobby Timmons and Betty Carter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: Bobby Timmons was of course the Adderley’s, this band’s first piano player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions 'Bobby Timmons' in a list of musicians; INTERVIEWEE responds generally about the period ('that was a period I think when everybody including me was trying to play that way') without describing a specific personal or professional relationship with Timmons.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
